pattern: The transpose simply re-reads the grid with row and column roles swapped, so the cell at output position (j, i) is the input cell (i, j).
hints: The result has as many rows as the input has columns, and as many columns as the input has rows.
Every value at row i, column j of the original lands at row j, column i of the answer.
Allocate an n-by-m grid (n columns of the input become rows) and copy input[i][j] into output[j][i] for every cell.

edge_inputs.description: second example: more columns than rows
single cell is its own transpose
single row becomes a single column
single column becomes a single row
negative and large values must move intact

stress.description: random rectangular matrix of varying shape and full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [matrix]
stress.note: independent m and n catch any row/column confusion
